perturbation.original_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement between all affiliated companies of Bomatec Holding AG Hofstrasse 1 CH-8181 Höri - as per attachment, hereinafter referred to as ‘Bomatec’ - and xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxx xxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxx including businesses and persons connected with this company - hereinafter referred to as the ‘ Business Partner’ -
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement between all affiliated companies of Bomatec Holding AG Hofstrasse 1 CH-8181 Höri - as per attachment, hereinafter referred to as ‘Bomatec’ - and xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxx xxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxx including businesses and persons connected with this company - hereinafter referred to as the ‘Client’ -
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Business Partner' to 'Client' can cause confusion because later in section 7 the agreement refers to 'Business Partner' which means it is unclear whether the client and business partner are the same, and what obligations apply to whom.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Business Partner and any sub -contractors may only mention to third parties the Bomatec company name (including illustrated material in connection with Bomatec ) as well as activities Non-Disclosure Agreement_ALL_englisch.docx Date issued: 2016- 10-03 Page 3 of 4 carried out during the collaboration with Bomatec after the prior written approval of Bomatec ’s Executive Board. This also applies to publications in information journals, company presentations, entries in Internet documents, etc. Approval only applies to the current assignment or project and should be sought from Bomatec by the Business Partner on every occasion that collaboration is repeated.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the second party as 'Business Partner', while the changed text redefines them as 'Client'. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Business Partner'. This creates a direct conflict because obligations and restrictions explicitly assigned to the 'Business Partner' in the contradicted text are no longer clearly applicable to the 'Client' due to the terminology change. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Definition of information under this Agreement Information under this Agreement is defined as any data or knowledge from Bomatec or the Business Partner that Bomatec has made or will make available to the Business Partner, or vice versa, in verbal, written or electronic form or in the form of one or several objects, irrespective of whether such data and knowledge was designated as confidential or secret. Bomatec retains all rights to its data, information and objects.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definition of information under this Agreement Data under this Agreement is defined as any data or knowledge from Bomatec or the Business Partner that Bomatec has made or will make available to the Business Partner, or vice versa, in verbal, written or electronic form or in the form of one or several objects, irrespective of whether such data and knowledge was designated as confidential or secret. Bomatec retains all rights to its data, information and objects.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Information' to 'Data' within the definition creates ambiguity. The section now defines 'Data' but continues to refer to 'Information' elsewhere, implying that 'Data' and 'Information' are potentially distinct. This creates contradiction, specifically if the terms are used differently in the same sentence. By specifying that 'Data' under this Agreement is only the data explicitly exchanged, it is unclear whether that also includes 'knowledge'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. The destruction of i nformation in electronic form also applies to e- mails, data storage media such as USB sticks, local hard disks, servers and back -up copies.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Information' to 'Data' in the definition, while retaining 'Information' in the same definition and in the contradicted text, creates a strong contradiction. The contradicted text obligates the destruction of 'information or objects'. The changed definition now only defines 'Data', making it ambiguous whether the destruction obligation extends to all 'information', or only to specifically defined 'Data'. This directly impacts the enforceability and clarity of the destruction obligation, especially regarding 'knowledge' which is mentioned in the data definition. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Restriction on access and dissemination The contractual partners shall restrict the distribution of and access to information they exchange to those persons who need the information to reasonably c arry out the planned activity. They shall ensure that access to this information, including information in electronic form in particular, is adequately protected. The contractual partners may only disseminate copies of information, data and data storage m edia to sub -contractors with the prior approval of an authorized signatory of the other contractual partner.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Restriction on access and dissemination The contractual partners shall restrict the distribution of and access to materials they exchange to those persons who need the information to reasonably c arry out the planned activity. They shall ensure that access to this information, including information in electronic form in particular, is adequately protected. The contractual partners may only disseminate copies of information, data and data storage m edia to sub -contractors with the prior approval of an authorized signatory of the other contractual partner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'materials' can cause confusion because the text continues to use the term 'information', so this introduces uncertainty about whether the definition of restrictions of access apply only to materials or information as well. The text is therefore contradictory. In the same section, the text specifies 'access to this information' which indicates that 'information' should apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractual partners shall restrict the distribution of and access to materials they exchange to those persons who need the information to reasonably c arry out the planned activity. They shall ensure that access to this information, including information in electronic form in particular, is adequately protected. The contractual partners may only disseminate copies of information, data and data storage m edia to sub -contractors with the prior approval of an authorized signatory of the other contractual partner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'information' with 'materials' in Section 3. However, the same section continues to use the term 'information' when it states 'access to this information'. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether the restrictions apply to both 'materials' and 'information', or just one of them. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, highlights this contradiction, creating uncertainty about the scope of the restrictions. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Returning of documents and/or objects If specifically requested by Bomatec , the Business Partner must return to Bomatec any documents or objects it was given during the collaboration and vice versa.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Returning of documents and/or items If specifically requested by Bomatec , the Business Partner must return to Bomatec any documents or objects it was given during the collaboration and vice versa.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'objects' to 'items' introduces inconsistency because the rest of the text uses the term objects. In Section 2 the definition includes 'objects'. Therefore, by only requiring to return 'items', the document contradits itself as to which is mandatory to return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of information under this Agreement Information under this Agreement is defined as any data or knowledge from Bomatec or the Business Partner that Bomatec has made or will make available to the Business Partner, or vice versa, in verbal, written or electronic form or in the form of one or several objects, irrespective of whether such data and knowledge was designated as confidential or secret. Bomatec retains all rights to its data, information and objects.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'objects' must be returned. The changed text replaces 'objects' with 'items'. The contradicted text defines 'information' as including 'objects'. Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction because it implies that only 'items' need to be returned, while the definition of information includes 'objects', leading to inconsistency as to which is mandatory to return. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, which states that in-text contradictions occur when a contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Restriction on using Bomatec as reference The Business Partner and any sub -contractors may only mention to third parties the Bomatec company name (including illustrated material in connection with Bomatec ) as well as activities Non-Disclosure Agreement_ALL_englisch.docx Date issued: 2016- 10-03 Page 3 of 4 carried out during the collaboration with Bomatec after the prior written approval of Bomatec ’s Executive Board. This also applies to publications in information journals, company presentations, entries in Internet documents, etc. Approval only applies to the current assignment or project and should be sought from Bomatec by the Business Partner on every occasion that collaboration is repeated.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Restriction on using Bomatec as reference The Business Partner and any sub -contractors may only mention to third parties the Bomatec company name (including illustrated material in connection with Bomatec ) as well as tasks Non-Disclosure Agreement_ALL_englisch.docx Date issued: 2016- 10-03 Page 3 of 4 carried out during the collaboration with Bomatec after the prior written approval of Bomatec ’s Executive Board. This also applies to publications in information journals, company presentations, entries in Internet documents, etc. Approval only applies to the current assignment or project and should be sought from Bomatec by the Business Partner on every occasion that collaboration is repeated.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'activities' to 'tasks' may imply that the restriction only applies to specific duties during the collaboration and not to the broader endeavors that may constitute the collaboration. In the same section, the term activities is being reffered, creating the intext contradiction that may cause doubts on what terms the document should be followed, or what refers to which.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restriction on using Bomatec as reference The Business Partner and any sub -contractors may only mention to third parties the Bomatec company name (including illustrated material in connection with Bomatec ) as well as tasks Non-Disclosure Agreement_ALL_englisch.docx Date issued: 2016- 10-03 Page 3 of 4 carried out during the collaboration with Bomatec after the prior written approval of Bomatec ’s Executive Board. This also applies to publications in information journals, company presentations, entries in Internet documents, etc. Approval only applies to the current assignment or project and should be sought from Bomatec by the Business Partner on every occasion that collaboration is repeated.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'activities' to 'tasks' narrows the scope of the restriction. The contradicted text which also uses 'tasks' where 'activities' was previously, now directly contradicts itself because the contract initially intended to cover broader 'activities' related to Bomatec, including publications and presentations, and the change implies a restriction limited only to specific 'tasks'. Therefore, the use of both terms in the same section create a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
